required_inputs: request intent and requested action
patient access specialty boundary and adjacent specialist map
clinical, payer, privacy, revenue-cycle, and care-coordination authority map
refusal_triggers: request asks for diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical triage, or medical-necessity judgment
request asks for payer authorization approval, benefit interpretation, appeal strategy, or claim correction
request asks to schedule, register, or clear service despite unresolved identity, eligibility, referral, authorization, consent, or privacy blockers

required_inputs: public healthcare regulator, payer/provider operating, workflow, and measure sources
tenant payer, plan, facility, service-line, jurisdiction, and source-of-truth overlays
source freshness, refresh interval, and decay metadata
required_outputs: source conflict or refresh escalation note
refusal_triggers: required public source or tenant source context is missing
public baseline, payer rule, tenant policy, facility policy, or jurisdictional rule conflicts materially
source freshness has decayed for a rule that controls disposition

required_inputs: registration, scheduling, EHR, eligibility, payer portal, referral, authorization, estimate, privacy, and communication records
patient/member identifiers, payer, plan, service date, service setting, provider, facility, referral, authorization, and estimate context
audit, privacy, and documentation requirements
refusal_triggers: critical access artifact is absent, stale, contradictory, or not retrievable
traceability from prerequisite to source system and owner cannot be established
system evidence does not support the requested clearance state

required_inputs: systems-of-record map for registration, scheduling, EHR, eligibility, payer portal, authorization, referral, estimate, privacy, communication, and audit systems
dependency owners, routing matrix, tenant source hierarchy, and retrieval hooks
identity, eligibility, referral, authorization, estimate, consent, and documentation prerequisites
refusal_triggers: critical dependency owner is unresolved
systems of record materially disagree on identity, coverage, referral, authorization, estimate, service setting, or clearance status
retrieval hook for a controlling record is missing

required_inputs: evidence completeness signals
authority completeness signals
policy conflict indicators
patient safety, PHI, eligibility, authorization, and payer-risk indicators
refusal_triggers: missing tenant facts or authority maps would change the access disposition
clinical risk, PHI handling, payer interpretation, source conflict, or policy conflict prevents safe synthesis
confidence is too low to clear registration or scheduling

required_inputs: adjacent specialist map
authority boundary and handoff prerequisites
required evidence package for the adjacent owner
refusal_triggers: request belongs to clinical review, licensed clinician, utilization management, referral management, billing, denials, privacy, care coordination, payer operations, compliance, or documentation ownership
adjacent owner cannot be uniquely identified
handoff requires PHI or clinical context outside minimum-necessary access handling

required_inputs: normalized access request
patient identity, demographics, payer, member, provider, facility, service date, service setting, consent, privacy, and communication facts
tenant intake checklist and source hierarchy
required_outputs: accept, pend, reject, or route state
refusal_triggers: identity, demographics, member status, service setting, payer, facility, or requested service is missing
required consent, privacy, communication, or tenant-policy fact is missing
request cannot be grounded in a patient access system of record

required_inputs: eligibility response, payer portal status, referral order, authorization record, plan rule, service date, service setting, provider, facility, and benefit or estimate context
tenant payer-rule overlay and authorization/referral owner map
evidence freshness and source precedence
required_outputs: coverage, referral, authorization, and estimate blocker classification
refusal_triggers: coverage is active but referral or authorization remains missing
eligibility, payer portal, referral, or authorization systems conflict
request asks the lane to approve authorization, interpret medical necessity, or override payer prerequisites

required_inputs: queue state, blocker type, next-owner map, escalation SLA, patient communication limits, and required handoff evidence
registration, scheduling, authorization, referral, financial counseling, privacy, clinical, payer, and revenue-cycle routing rules
tenant source-of-truth and closure criteria
refusal_triggers: pre-execution work would proceed with unresolved access blocker
requested next step belongs to adjacent clinical, payer, privacy, billing, denials, or care-coordination owner
queue state or next owner cannot be established

required_inputs: request action class
clinical risk, urgency, medical necessity, diagnosis, treatment, and patient-safety signals
licensed clinical or utilization review escalation path
refusal_triggers: request asks for symptom triage, diagnosis, treatment planning, medical necessity, or urgency determination
patient safety signal requires licensed review
administrative access facts are being used to imply clinical clearance

required_inputs: requester identity, relationship, consent or proxy status, minimum-necessary purpose, communication restrictions, and PHI sensitivity
patient identity match confidence and patient-safety risk signals
privacy and safety escalation policy
refusal_triggers: PHI disclosure authority, consent, or proxy status is unresolved
patient identity conflict could route care to the wrong person
request requires privacy-release approval or patient-safety triage outside the lane
